8.reason: [If the file does not exist, an IOException is thrown, causing the program to terminate without any output. The program should handle the absence of the file or the environment must provide it, but the current handling with RuntimeException does not produce the required output.]

The program should handle the absence of the file or the environment must provide it, but the current handling with RuntimeException does not produce the required output.]

79.reason: [The regex requires a newline at the end, but if the comment is at the end of the file without a trailing newline, it will not be matched, causing the lexer to not skip the comment and potentially fail to process correctly.]

85.reason: The character class [ !#-&(-\\[\\]-~] is incomplete and misses many valid printable ASCII characters (e.g., '*', '+', ',', '-', '.', '/', ':', ';', '<', '=', '>', '?', '@', '^', '_', '`', '''), causing valid string constants to fail matching.

81.reason: Requires a trailing newline to match, so it will fail to skip single-line comments at the end of the file. Should be "//[^\n]*" or "//.*".

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Requires a trailing newline to match, so it will fail to skip single-line comments at the end of the file. Should be "//[^\n]*" or "//.*".

82.reason: Uses greedy quantifier '*', which may incorrectly consume multiple comments or code between '/*' and '*/'. Should use non-greedy '*?'.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Uses greedy quantifier '*', which may incorrectly consume multiple comments or code between '/*' and '*/'. Should use non-greedy '*?'.

130.reason: The regex requires a trailing newline to match, which will fail to skip single-line comments at the end of the file if no newline follows. It should be "//[^\n]*" or "//.*?(?=\\n|$)".

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The regex requires a trailing newline to match, which will fail to skip single-line comments at the end of the file if no newline follows. It should be "//[^\n]*" or "//.*?(?=\\n|$)".

131.reason: The regex uses a greedy quantifier (*), causing it to match from the first "/*" to the very last "*/" in the entire file, incorrectly swallowing valid code between multiple comments. It should be non-greedy: "/\\*[\\s\\S]*?\\*/".

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The regex uses a greedy quantifier (*), causing it to match from the first "/*" to the very last "*/" in the entire file, incorrectly swallowing valid code between multiple comments. It should be non-greedy: "/\\*[\\s\\S]*?\\*/".

169.reason: [The error patterns for single '&' and '|' should be checked immediately after the corresponding multi-character operators (&&, ||) to ensure they are recognized as errors when not part of the valid operators. Although the current placement at the end may work in theory, in practice, if any subsequent pattern accidentally matches (which shouldn't happen), it could cause a missed error. However, the real issue is that the current order is correct, but given the test failure, this is the only suspicious location.]
